title: Using a tool library: what's available, how to join, and what to borrow vs buy
subtitle: Finding UK tool libraries, typical membership costs, and the economics of borrowing rarely-used tools
excerpt: A tool library is a community loan service for tools and equipment. The UK has around 100 active tool libraries, most run by charities or community groups. Membership typically costs a modest cost. A cordless drill used four times a year at a modest cost to buy has a 30-year payback on the purchase; borrowing it from a library for a modest cost is clearly better value for occasional users.
sourceNotes: Share and Repair UK tool library directory. Tool libraries movement mapping project (2022) for UK coverage data. WRAP circular economy data on tool ownership and usage rates. Which? DIY guide to occasional tool hire vs purchase economics. Library of Things case study data from London and Edinburgh pilot programmes.

content.text: Most tools sit unused for over 95% of their lifespan. A power drill, bought for one bathroom tiling project, may be used 8 times in its 20-year life. A tool library keeps the same drill available to dozens of households, reducing the total number manufactured and cutting the per-use cost for everyone. UK tool libraries typically charge a modest cost membership or a modest cost, with loan periods of 1-7 days depending on the item.

Library of Things operates in several London boroughs and Edinburgh. Share and Repair has a directory at shareandrepair.net. Many Transition Town groups run informal tool-lending through community Facebook groups or Freegle. The Leeds Library of Things is the oldest continuously operating UK service. Search your postcode on the Share and Repair directory first; if nothing appears within 5 miles, check your local Transition Town group and community garden networks.
Power tools: cordless drill-driver, jigsaw, circular saw, sander, heat gun, tile cutter
Garden tools: lawn aerator, rotavator, hedge trimmer, pressure washer, leaf blower
Ladders and access: step ladder, extension ladder, roof ladder
Event equipment: folding tables, chairs, gazebos, PA systems, projectors
Specialist DIY: tile stripper, floorboard lifter, concrete mixer, drain rods

Borrow if used fewer than 4 times per year: most power tools, pressure washers, ladders over 3 m, rotavators, concrete mixers. The cost to buy divided by use occasions typically puts borrowing well ahead of purchasing.

Buy if used weekly or in very short-notice situations: a standard hand screwdriver, a tape measure, scissors, a hammer. Items used too frequently or needed instantly on a Sunday afternoon when the library is closed.
Starting a tool library if none exists nearby

Library of Things and Share and Repair both offer startup guides for new initiatives. The minimum needed is a spreadsheet, storage space, and a few donated tools. Many libraries start with 10-20 items and grow through donations from downsizing households. The startup guide from Library of Things is free to download and covers liability insurance (typically through a community organisational umbrella), tool checking procedures, and loan agreement templates.
